finding: When a governed commit is blocked by `ATM_TOUCHED_PHYSICAL_LINE_BUDGET_BLOCKED`, ATM reports only the wrapper-level `ATM_GIT_COMMIT_FAILED` in the operator flow and leaves the captain to inspect `.atm/runtime/git-commit-attempts/*` plus manually reconstruct a minimal patch from a formatter-contaminated file.
expectedBehavior: The governed commit path should surface the nested line-budget error, affected files, numstat, and a copyable recovery path. For formatter or bulk-rewrite contamination, ATM should offer a first-class minimal-patch rebuild or split-policy recovery that preserves intended semantic hunks without requiring each captain to rediscover the same procedure.
evidenceOrRepro: During ATM-GOV-0258, `node atm.mjs git commit --actor antigravity-gemini35-plan31-captain --task ATM-GOV-0258 --message "fix(tasks): preserve dirty release wip safely" --json` failed as `ATM_GIT_COMMIT_FAILED` with empty stdout/stderr. The attempt file `.atm/runtime/git-commit-attempts/antigravity-gemini35-plan31-captain__ATM-GOV-0258.json` revealed `errorCode: "ATM_TOUCHED_PHYSICAL_LINE_BUDGET_BLOCKED"`. `git diff --numstat -- packages/cli/src/commands/git-governance/implementation.ts` showed `5404 insertions / 5 deletions`, caused by formatter-style full-file expansion. A manual captain pass rebuilt the file from `HEAD:path` and reapplied only the `--wip` semantic hunks, reducing that file to `1 insertion / 1 deletion` while focused validators still passed.
followUp: Add an ATM recovery surface for nested commit failure diagnosis and formatter-contaminated minimal-patch rebuild guidance. Register exact error-code entries for `ATM_GIT_COMMIT_FAILED` and `ATM_TOUCHED_PHYSICAL_LINE_BUDGET_BLOCKED`, and link the behavior to Plan 3.1 evidence so future captains get deterministic recovery instead of manual forensic work.
